summary: Live-verified endpoint that returns paginated federal account funding rows for a single award, with reporting-period, agency, object class, and program activity context. This bundle normalizes the cross-endpoint generated award hash as canonical_award_lookup_id: search emits generated_internal_id, award detail echoes generated_unique_award_id, and funding accepts the same value under request body award_id. For safe pagination and comparison workflows, treat explicit sort plus order as the primary request pattern because omitted-sort behavior is contradicted. Funding rows can be sparse accounting slices and may align more closely with award-detail total_account_obligation than headline total_obligation.
businessPurpose: Use this endpoint to explain how one award is funded across federal accounts and reporting periods, including obligated and outlay amounts and the agencies and accounting classifications attached to each funding row. It is the funding-composition follow-up step after capturing canonical_award_lookup_id from award search or award detail, not the authoritative headline-award-total endpoint.
analyticalGrain: One row per award funding/accounting slice for a specific reporting fiscal period and agency/account/program-object-class combination within a single requested award.

primaryEntities.name: Award
primaryEntities.description: The endpoint is scoped to exactly one requested canonical_award_lookup_id, carried under request body field award_id for this route.

primaryEntities.name: Federal account funding row
primaryEntities.description: Each result row represents a funding-related accounting slice attached to the award, identified by federal account, agencies, object class, program activity, and reporting period fields.

measures.name: Transaction obligated amount
measures.description: Obligated amount reported for the funding row.

measures.name: Gross outlay amount
measures.description: Gross outlay amount reported for the funding row; live data can include null values.

dimensions.name: Reporting fiscal period
dimensions.description: Rows are labeled by reporting fiscal year, quarter, and month, plus whether the submission is quarterly.

dimensions.name: Federal account
dimensions.description: Rows include federal_account and account_title to identify the account receiving or providing funding context.

dimensions.name: Funding and awarding agencies
dimensions.description: Rows include both funding and awarding agency ids, names, top-tier ids, and slugs.

dimensions.name: Program activity and object class
dimensions.description: Rows include program activity code/name and object class code/name so callers can explain how funding is classified within the award.

dimensions.name: Canonical award lookup id
dimensions.description: Normalize the cross-endpoint handoff identifier as canonical_award_lookup_id: v2__search__spending_by_award emits generated_internal_id, v2__awards__award_id echoes generated_unique_award_id, and this endpoint expects the same value under request body field award_id.

dimensions.name: Disaster/emergency fund code
dimensions.description: Rows include disaster_emergency_fund_code as a raw code/string-or-null field. Preserve the returned code and avoid boolean-style parsing because docs type it inconsistently while live and reviewer-backed evidence returned code values or null.

suitableQuestions.name: Which federal accounts and agencies are associated with a given award's funding rows?
suitableQuestions.description: The endpoint is well suited for inspecting federal_account, account_title, and the awarding/funding agency context for one award.

suitableQuestions.name: How do obligated and outlay amounts vary across reporting periods for one award?
suitableQuestions.description: Use reporting_fiscal_year/quarter/month together with transaction_obligated_amount and gross_outlay_amount to study time-sliced funding activity for one award.

suitableQuestions.name: Which object classes or program activities appear in the funding history of one award?
suitableQuestions.description: The endpoint returns object class and program activity fields on each row, enabling classification-oriented inspection for a single award.

suitableQuestions.name: What funding rows belong to the same award I already selected in search or award detail?
suitableQuestions.description: Use canonical_award_lookup_id to carry one award cleanly from spending_by_award or award detail into funding composition analysis without inferring endpoint-specific identifier labels.

notSuitableFor.name: Searching across many awards
notSuitableFor.description: This endpoint requires one award_id and does not provide a multi-award search surface.

notSuitableFor.name: Computing a complete count of funding rows before paging
notSuitableFor.description: Neither the docs nor the current raw profile indicate a total-count field in page_metadata.

notSuitableFor.name: Treating results as a simple award total without grain checks
notSuitableFor.description: Rows are accounting slices over time and classification dimensions, so naive aggregation can misrepresent the award if the caller assumes each row is a unique total snapshot.

notSuitableFor.name: Blindly joining from the display Award ID in search results
notSuitableFor.description: The human-facing Award ID from spending_by_award is not the preferred machine handoff key when a generated helper id is available; normalize the generated value as canonical_award_lookup_id instead.

notSuitableFor.name: Reconciling directly to headline award totals from award detail
notSuitableFor.description: Funding results can be sparse accounting slices rather than a restatement of total_obligation. In the reviewer-backed HT940216C0001 drilldown, award detail reported total_obligation 51269205263.03 while funding returned one 321840 row with hasNext false, much closer to total_account_obligation 321840.

joins.description: Normalize these related labels as the semantic alias canonical_award_lookup_id. In v2__search__spending_by_award the alias value is generated_internal_id; in v2__awards__award_id it is echoed as generated_unique_award_id; send the same string unchanged here under request body field award_id.

joins.to: Agency reference data or agency-focused USAspending endpoints
joins.description: Agency identifiers and slugs in each row can be aligned with agency reference surfaces when the caller needs hierarchy or display metadata beyond this endpoint.

workflows.name: Inspect funding composition for one known award
workflows.description: Given one normalized award handoff identifier, page through funding rows to explain account, agency, and reporting-period context for that same award.

steps.action: Obtain canonical_award_lookup_id. If chaining from v2__search__spending_by_award, use generated_internal_id; if chaining from v2__awards__award_id, use generated_unique_award_id rather than the human-facing display Award ID.

Send canonical_award_lookup_id under request body field award_id together with the primary safe template shape {page, limit, sort, order}, then call POST /api/v2/awards/funding/; reviewer-backed drilldowns confirmed the same generated hash carried successfully into this route and succeeded with explicit sort=reporting_fiscal_date and order=desc.

Interpret each row as a funding/accounting slice for that same canonical_award_lookup_id and continue paging with page_metadata.next or hasNext until the needed coverage is collected; the reviewer-confirmed safe-template call echoed page_metadata.page=1, but sparse responses with hasNext=false can still be legitimate for large awards.

Treat explicit sort plus order as the safe default workflow whenever row order matters. The contradiction is in omitted default sort behavior rather than in explicit documented sort values; reviewer-backed safe-template calls succeeded with sort=reporting_fiscal_date and order=desc.

If you compare funding to award detail, treat funding as accounting-slice evidence. Reviewer-backed drilldown showed a case where funding aligned with total_account_obligation 321840 and not with total_obligation 51269205263.03, so sparse rows should trigger interpretation caution rather than automatic mismatch assumptions.

caveats.statement: Related award-search, award-detail, and award-funding endpoints relabel the same generated award business key. Normalize it as canonical_award_lookup_id: search emits generated_internal_id, award detail echoes generated_unique_award_id, and this endpoint accepts the same value under request body field award_id.
caveats.impact: Carry the generated identifier unchanged across endpoints and do not substitute the human-facing display Award ID when a generated helper id is available.

caveats.statement: The documented default sort conflicts with live and reviewer-backed behavior when sort is omitted; explicit documented sort values remain the safe primary pattern.
caveats.impact: Treat omitted sort as unsafe for deterministic pagination or comparisons and prefer explicit sort plus order.

caveats.statement: Unknown award ids may return HTTP 200 with an empty results array according to the current raw profile, even though this bundle did not re-probe that case.
caveats.impact: Empty results should not be treated as proof that the request was semantically valid without validating the award id elsewhere.

caveats.statement: The meaning of disaster_emergency_fund_code letters is not fully documented, and the field type is contradicted by live and reviewer-backed evidence.
caveats.impact: Model the field as a raw code/string-or-null value, preserve the returned code, and avoid boolean-style parsing or business labeling without outside corroboration.

caveats.statement: Gross outlay amounts may be null on otherwise valid rows.
caveats.impact: Aggregation and display logic should handle null outlays explicitly.

caveats.statement: Funding results can be sparse and may not reconcile to the headline award total_obligation. In a reviewer-backed HT940216C0001 drilldown, award detail listed total_obligation 51269205263.03 and total_account_obligation 321840, while funding returned one 321840 row with hasNext false.
caveats.impact: Use this endpoint to explain funding composition and accounting context. When comparing with award detail, first check whether total_account_obligation is the closer comparison point rather than expecting total_obligation reconciliation.
